exercises.title: Matching Graph Builder
exercises.description: Build a matching graph from surface code syndrome data.
tasks: Input: 2D syndrome array from surface code
Identify all defects (non-zero syndromes)
Create complete graph with Manhattan distance weights
Add boundary nodes with distance to nearest boundary
Output: adjacency matrix with weights
hints: Boundary distance = min to any edge

exercises.title: Simple MWPM Solver
exercises.description: Implement a basic MWPM solver for small graphs.
tasks: For graphs with ≤6 vertices, enumerate all matchings
Calculate weight of each perfect matching
Return minimum weight matching
Test on example defect configurations
Compare to library implementation
hints: Perfect matching: every vertex matched exactly once
Use recursion or itertools for enumeration

exercises.title: 3D Matching Extension
exercises.description: Extend matching graph to handle measurement errors.
tasks: Input: syndrome history over d rounds
Detect defects as syndrome changes between rounds
Add temporal edges with weight 1
Add spatial edges with distance weights
Solve 3D matching problem
hints: Defect at (x, y, t) if syndrome changes at (x,y) at time t

exercises.title: Threshold Estimator
exercises.description: Estimate MWPM decoder threshold through simulation.
tasks: Simulate surface code with random errors
Run MWPM decoder on syndromes
Determine success/failure (logical error?)
Vary physical error rate and code distance
Find threshold crossing point
hints: Logical error: correction × error = logical operator
Threshold: where p_L vs d curves cross
Need many samples per (p, d) point
